channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: « Flash charging » : BYD dévoile la technologie de recharge de voiture électrique aussi rapide qu’un plein d’essence
item.description: Recharger une voiture électrique BYD prend autant de temps que faire le plein de sa voiture essence. C'est ce que vient de dévoiler le géant chinois. Une technologie de recharge en 5 minutes qui devrait arriver en Europe à terme.

item.title: Wizards of the Coast Is Officially Calling It ‘Dungeons & Dragons’ 5.5E, Would Like You to Not Read Anything Into That
item.description: After years of insisting otherwise, the TTRPG publisher has thrown its hands up and called the latest iteration of 'D&D' what people have been calling it since day one.

item.title: Best Gravel Running Shoes (2026): Salomon, Adidas, Nike
item.description: The best new shoes in this hybrid, niche footwear style can take you from your front door, down the asphalt, and into the trees.

item.title: Rivian is betting its future on one of the fastest EV launches in US history
item.description: If Rivian reaches its R2 sales target in 2026, it will be one of the fastest ramp-ups ever of a new EV in the United States.

item.title: Lio raises $30M from Andreessen Horowitz and others to automate enterprise procurement
item.description: AI procurement startup Lio announced a $30 million Series A in a round led by Andreessen Horowitz.

item.title: Formula 1 2026 regulations explained: New power, aero and strategy era begins
item.description: Formula 1’s 2026 regulations bring new power units, active aero, smaller cars, and sustainable fuels. Here’s how the rule changes will reshape racing.

item.title: BMW’s i3 prototype conquers the ice with power and technology
item.description: For an electric car to survive in this incentive-free, tariff-laden, emissions-loving world, it has to be very, very good. It also helps if it's priced right, and looking great doesn't hurt either. Unfortunately for BMW's latest EV, the i3 sedan, we still can't say much about those last two questions. BMW hasn't announced pricing yet, and thanks to some eye-crossing camouflage, it's impossible to know exactly what it looks like, either. But, after a day behind the wheel of a prototype machine sliding it through the Swedish wilds, I can at least confidently confirm that it's shaping up to be a ...

item.title: Anthropic is reportedly back in talks with the Defense Department
item.description: Anthropic is reportedly trying to reach a new deal with the US Defense Department, which could prevent the government from labeling it a supply chain risk. According to Financial Times and Bloomberg, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has resumed talks with the agency over the use of its AI models. In particular, the publications say that Amodel is having discussions with Emil Michael, the Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering.The two of them were trying to work out the contract over the use of Anthropic’s models before negotiations broke down and the government soured on the compan...

item.title: Vous avez une question sur votre santé féminine ? Votre bague Oura pourra bientôt vous répondre
item.description: Connues notamment pour leur suivi de santé général, les bagues connectées Oura Ring se tournent désormais vers le suivi de la santé féminine grâce à l’introduction dans son application d’un chatbot IA qui devrait vous permettre de répondre à toutes vos questions.

item.title: Dutch MPs call for free government-backed VPN, ad-blocker, and password manager for all citizens
item.description: Following major data leaks at Odido and health services, a motion tabled by the Dutch opposition argues that online safety shouldn't depend on your income and calls on the government to hand every citizen a free "digital first-aid kit."

item.title: IKEA's tiny, stackable Kallsup speakers let you 'build your own sonic skyscraper', and they're on sale now for $10 each
item.description: Grab as many of these punchy little cubes as your budget will allow, and start building your own 'sonic skyscraper'.

item.title: Cette image de l’éclipse de Lune n’est pas comme les autres, et la Nasa s’y intéresse de près
item.description: Elle était la dernière avant fin 2028. L’éclipse totale de Lune de ce mardi 3 mars 2026 a tenu ses promesses. Et un peu plus, même. La preuve avec cette image jamais vue…

item.title: Nothing Phone 4a Pro hands-on: A premium design with a price to match the Pixel 10a
item.description: Nothing has announced its latest premium midrange device, the Phone 4a Pro. The company says it's the thinnest full-metal phone on the market, measuring in at 7.95mm. It also looks notably different from the prior A-series phones – and pretty much any of the company's phones to date. It features an aluminum unibody while retaining Nothing’s retro-clear hardware design touches, with a clear, redesigned camera unit. Yes, the aggressively protruding circular camera unit of the Phone 3a Pro is gone, replaced with an oblong housing that houses the triple-camera array and a tweaked Glyph Matrix, sim...

item.title: Best Workout Headphones (2026), Tested in the Gym and in the Streets
item.description: Rock your inner jock with a pair of sturdy, sweatproof, and tangle-proof headphones. Here are our favorites.

item.title: Apple Music is flagging AI slop before Spotify has even started — but there's a catch
item.description: Apple Music is rolling out Transparency Tags for AI-generated music, but the responsibility lies with record labels.

item.title: Nothing sees your colorful MacBook Neo, and raises you two new funky Android phones — meet the Phone (4a) and (4a) Pro
item.description: The Nothing Phone (4a) and Phone (4a) Pro have just been unveiled alongside the affordable Nothing Headphone (a) — and they come in some funky colors.

item.title: You can get the MacBook Neo for just $499 / £499 with this easy-to-miss education discount at Apple
item.description: Apple's official education store is a gold mine for discounts if you're eligible—even on new models like the latest MacBook Neo.

item.title: Avec 500 € de réduction, ce VTC électrique Intersport avec 100 km d’autonomie s’impose comme un VAE de choix
item.description: Par le biais de sa marque Nakamura, Intersport propose le VTC Crossover V LTD, un modèle pour les cyclistes urbains ou périurbains en quête de polyvalence, pour 1 399,99 euros au lieu de 1 899,99 euros.

item.title: Apple Music can now flag AI content, but only if distributors elect to label it
item.description: While music streaming apps like Bandcamp, Spotify and Deezer have taken steps to inform users about AI-generated content, we haven't heard much out of Apple Music in that regard. However, Apple Music has now introduced "Transparency Tags" designed to show listeners if any elements were generated in whole or part by AI. The catch is that Apple is leaving it up to labels and distributors to create those tags, according to an Apple newsletter to industry partners seen by Music Business Worldwide.. "Proper tagging of content is the first step in giving the music industry the data and tools neede...

item.title: Ils ont créé une IA pour remplacer leur patron, la folle idée des ingénieurs d’Uber
item.description: L'intelligence artificielle repousse sans cesse les limites du monde de l'entreprise. Chez le géant des VTC, les développeurs ont carrément mis au point une version virtuelle de leur dirigeant pour s'entraîner avant leurs présentations officielles.

item.title: Nothing Headphone (a) : un casque Bluetooth plus abordable, à l’autonomie record et haut en couleurs pour s’attaquer aux meilleurs
item.description: Nothing annonce officiellement son nouveau casque Headphone (a), un modèle Bluetooth circum-aural proposé à 159 euros et pensé pour élargir sa clientèle en conservant l’ADN du Headphone (1) tout en misant sur une autonomie record et des coloris audacieux pour concurrencer Sony et JBL sur le milieu de gamme.

item.title: Ubisoft provides updates on the Assassin's Creed series, says its 'taking the time to deliver on its ambitious vision' of Hexe and Codename Invictus is 'a new approach to multiplayer in the franchise, but it isn't quite what the rumors have suggested'
item.description: Ubisoft has shared new details about the future of the Assassin's Creed franchise and announced a new 60FPS update for Assassin's Creed Unity.

item.title: Bill Gates’ TerraPower Finally Has A Permit for a Nuclear Reactor, but No Reliable Way to Fuel It
item.description: Commercial quantities of the required fuel are currently only available from one vendor: the Russian state.

item.title: Why the Monsters of ‘The Bride!’ Don’t Look That Monstrous
item.description: Maggie Gyllenhaal explained her creature-creation philosophy when it came to designing looks for Christian Bale and Jessie Buckley in her new movie.

item.title: KEF Muo Bluetooth Speaker: Portable Hi-Fi
item.description: Is a $250 Bluetooth speaker worth it? If it's this one made by KEF, it just might be.

item.title: 'Email is at an inflection point': new study finds only 13% of emails sent worldwide were actually written by humans
item.description: Tired of junk mail? You're not alone – 87% of emails are actually generated by automated systems and aren't personal.

item.title: Tai Chi walking: Why ’meditation in motion’ has taken over TikTok
item.description: Forget 10,000 steps—TikTok users are sharing an easy way to incorporate a centuries-old Chinese martial art into your exercise routine. There’s a new exercise trend making the rounds on the internet: Tai Chi walking, inspired by a centuries-old Chinese martial art that incorporates flowing hand and foot movements with breath and mindfulness.

item.title: James Talarico’s vintage-print-inspired type signals a new political landscape
item.description: Imperfect lettering in campaign materials offers a refreshing contrast to the corporate-style Democratic branding of the past. Texas state Representative James Talarcio won Tuesday’s Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, but his campaign’s real mission is even more ambitious. “We are not just trying to win an election. We are trying to fundamentally change our politics,” Talarico said during his election night speech in South Austin, where he touted the more than 28,000 volunteers the campaign recruited and the impressive amount of funding it raised without taking money from corporate PACs....

item.title: Anthropic makes last-ditch effort to salvage deal with Pentagon after blowup
item.description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei is reportedly back at the negotiating table with the Department of Defense in an attempt to salvage the company's relationship with the US military and prevent it from being iced out of defense work for being a "supply chain risk." Talks between the two parties imploded on Friday after weeks of […]

item.title: Security researchers found 'critical' flaw in IPVanish Mac VPN app — here's all you need to know
item.description: A newly discovered high-severity vulnerability in the IPVanish macOS app allows local attackers to hijack the VPN's background processes, granting them root privileges without the user ever knowing.

item.title: Fini la simple box internet : Orange veut enfin transformer sa Livebox en vrai mini-ordinateur, on pu tester prplOS
item.description: Orange s'apprête enfin à transformer nos box en mini-ordinateurs avec le Livebox Store, une boutique d'applications qui pourrait changer votre quotidien.

item.title: How to get funded in 2026 if you’re not an AI startup in San Francisco
item.description: If you’re building outside the AI hype storm, here’s what you need to know. Raising venture capital for a physical-world company can feel harder than getting struck by lightning. You could be standing on a mountain for months, holding a metal pole in a storm, waiting. And you probably still wouldn’t get hit.

item.title: Dreame va s’inviter dans votre cuisine et votre salon en 2026 : voici ses nouveaux appareils connectés [Sponso]
item.description: Dreame voit grand pour la France en 2026 ! Bien connue pour ses aspirateurs robots, la marque lance cette année de nouvelles références, mais dans le domaine du gros électroménager.

item.title: Nothing Headphone A Review: A Little More Colorful and a Little Less Good
item.description: The Headphone A may be $100 less than the Headphone 1, but there are some compromises to consider.

item.title: Suunto Vertical 2 Review: Catching Up to the Pack
item.description: Suunto's latest adventure fitness tracker finally introduces a bright AMOLED screen that's great for maps.

item.title: The quarry that built modern Beijing gets a surprising second life
item.description: Huge pits that supplied the concrete for a Chinese megacity have been revitalized into a striking park. On the northern outskirts of Beijing, massive holes in the earth bear the scars of what it’s taken to fuel the Chinese capital’s growth into a sprawling megacity that more than 22 million people call home.

item.title: Amazon fait -40 % sur de bons petits écouteurs Huawei qui misent sur le confort et l’autonomie
item.description: Les Huawei FreeBuds SE 3 sont des écouteurs boutons légers, d'entrée de gamme, avec un accent sur le confort et la praticité plutôt que sur l'audio pur. Ils passent de 49,99 euros à 29,99 euros sur Amazon.

item.title: Microsoft Teams wants to make it easier to forget all your embarrassing meeting mistakes
item.description: Microsoft Teams meeting content can now be quickly deleted.

item.title: Nothing présente son casque le plus accessible, le Nothing Headphone (a)
item.description: La marque britannique Nothing dévoile aujourd’hui son nouveau casque audio, le Nothing Headphone (a), disponible dès maintenant au prix de 159 euros. Un modèle plus abordable qui promet jusqu’à 135 heures d’écoute.

item.title: This Absurdly Complex Star Destroyer Model May Be the Most Detailed Ever Created
item.description: It has 172,340 pieces and just under 1.4 billion triangles, and it induced a panic attack in our copy of Blender.

item.title: Nothing Phone (4a) et (4a) Pro : des nouveaux coloris et une Glyph Bar revisitée
item.description: Nothing annonce aujourd’hui la sortie de deux nouveaux smartphones, le Nothing Phone (4a) et le Nothing Phone (4a) Pro. Deux modèles de milieu de gamme qui misent sur un design retravaillé sans rogner sur les performances.

item.title: Nothing Now Has a Cheaper Version of One of 2025’s Best Wireless Headphones
item.description: The Headphone A come in new colors, but not all of them are here to stay.

item.title: Nothing Phone 4a hands-on: A more technically accomplished entry-level smartphone
item.description: Nothing is back with two new smartphones, the entry-level Phone 4a and the mid-range Phone 4a Pro. With the base 4a in particular, there’s no shortage of substantial hardware upgrades since the 3a, even if the design doesn’t quite stand out as much. This year, that’s apparently the role of the Nothing Phone 4a Pro. That said, the base 4a’s two new color options (blue and pink) are gorgeous additions to the usual monochrome duo of white and black. There have been many upgrades since 2025’s Phone 3a. It comes with IP64 dust and water resistance, and is also physically tougher: Nothing says it ha...

item.title: Nothing's new over-ear headphones can pump out the jams for five days on a single charge
item.description: Nothing just revealed a new pair of over-ear headphones as a relatively budget-friendly alternative to the pre-existing Headphone 1. The Headphone (a) earphones feature a similar design language to the company's previous cans, but with more color. As a matter of fact, these will be available in four colors, including yellow, pink, white and black. Perhaps the most notable feature here, however, is the battery life. Nothing says these headphones will last over five days on a single charge. That's not five days of regular use, working out to several hours each day or whatever. It claims 135 hour...

item.title: Nothing Has 2 New Midrange Phones, but Only 1 Will Be Sold in the US
item.description: Nothing won't launch a flagship phone this year, but the colorful and affordable Phone (4a), Phone (4a) Pro, and Headphone (a) are here to fill the void.

item.title: Nothing’s Headphone A are something worth considering
item.description: After the release of the Headphone 1 last year - Nothing's first over-ear headphones - the company is back with the Headphone A. They're a slimmed-down over-ear option that keep the Nothing design aesthetic and excellent controls while decreasing the weight and bumping battery life up to a maximum of 135 hours. There are some […]

item.title: Nothing is finally covering up with the metal Phone 4A Pro
item.description: Today Nothing has revealed the 4A and 4A Pro, its latest midrange phones. The two look startlingly different from one another, with varying designs and Glyph Light interfaces. The Pro is especially striking: it's the first Nothing phone to almost entirely abandon the brand's trademark transparent design. I'm currently at Nothing's launch event in London, […]...

item.title: I reviewed Nothing's new budget phone, and it's a sleek, steady performer that impressed me so much that I might not go back to my Samsung
item.description: Nothing's new budget-friendly mobile wowed me with its stand-out design and impressive performance, making it easy to recommend.

item.title: The Nothing Headphone (a) are here, and they rock — here are 5 things you need to know about them
item.description: Nothing's new noise-cancelling headphones are half the price of the Headphone (1), but far from half the quality

item.title: I didn't think it could be done — the Nothing Headphone (a) fix all the problems of the original while being way cheaper
item.description: Nothing's new cheaper headphones are the dream version 2.0 of a product — better and way cheaper than the original

item.title: Nothing's Phone 4a Pro picks up flagship features and an even brighter display for $499
item.description: Back in January, Nothing shared that it wouldn't be releasing its flagship Nothing Phone 4 in 2026, and instead focusing on follow-ups to its midrange Phone 3a and Phone 3a Pro. After some expected teasing, those sequels have arrived: the Phone 4a Pro and Phone 4a. And like Nothing's previous devices, they seem like meaningful departures from what the company has tried in the past.The Nothing 4a Pro represents the biggest change. Gone is the translucent back meant to offer a pseudo-glimpse into the internals of the phone, and in its place is a metal unibody design in black, silver or pink, and...

item.title: Un zoom périscopique à 369 euros, on a testé le Nothing Phone (4a)
item.description: Avec sa gamme « a », Nothing a su bousculer les codes du milieu de gamme en alliant design clivant et performances équilibrées. Le nouveau Nothing Phone (4a) entend transformer l'essai en musclant sa partition photographique, notamment grâce à l'héritage du zoom périscopique de la version Pro.

item.title: Un châssis en métal comme l’iPhone, mais à moins de 500€ : Nothing frappe très fort avec le Phone (4a) Pro
item.description: Nothing dévoile le Phone (4a) Pro, un smartphone qui change radicalement son approche avec un design en métal unibody et le zoom périscopique du plus onéreux Phone (3).
